title: WordPress SEO Content Assistant (Australia, Premium Beverage Brand)
description:
We are an Australian premium beverage brand using WordPress as our website platform. We want to increase our Google traffic and online sales by improving our SEO and publishing high-quality content that targets real search terms (cocktails, mixers, tonic water, soda pairings, etc.).



We are hiring a WordPress SEO Content Assistant who can work independently and deliver weekly SEO improvements and content.



This is not a general VA role. We need someone with real SEO execution experience.



Goals of the Role (What Success Looks Like)



Within 30–90 days, we want to see:



More pages indexed in Google



More impressions and clicks in Google Search Console



Improved rankings for targeted keywords



Consistent publishing of SEO content



Organic traffic increasing month-on-month



SEO content driving clicks to product pages (sales intent)



SEO takes time, but we expect clear weekly outputs and measurable progress.



Platforms & Tools



You will work in:



WordPress (must have prior experience)



RankMath or Yoast SEO (RankMath preferred)



Google Search Console (must know how to use)



Google Analytics (basic reporting)



Keyword research tools (free tools are fine if you know what you’re doing)



Core Responsibilities

1) SEO Content Production



Create SEO content that targets keywords related to:



premium tonic water



best tonic for gin



cocktail mixers



low sugar mixers



cocktail recipes (GIN, VODKA, TEQUILA, SPRITZ, etc.)



Write content that is:



helpful, structured, and easy to read



optimised for SEO



includes internal links to our product pages



includes clear calls-to-action (shop now, buy online, etc.)



2) On-Page SEO Optimisation



Optimise existing WordPress pages by improving:



SEO titles and meta descriptions



headings (H1/H2/H3 structure)



internal linking



image optimisation (alt text, file names, compression)



readability and keyword placement



3) Technical SEO Basics (WordPress-level)



Identify and fix common WordPress SEO issues such as:



missing meta titles/descriptions



broken links



duplicate content



missing alt tags



pages not indexed



poor internal linking



Ensure:



sitemap is working



Search Console is configured properly



important pages are indexed



4) SEO Reporting & Weekly Updates



Provide a simple weekly report with:



work completed



links to pages created/updated



target keywords used



Search Console progress (impressions/clicks/indexing updates)



next week’s plan



Weekly Deliverables (Non-Negotiable)



You will work 6 hours per week to start.



Each week you must deliver:



Deliverable A: SEO Content (1 article per week)



1 x SEO blog post published to WordPress



1,000 to 1,500 words



Includes:



keyword target + 2–5 related keywords



SEO title + meta description



internal links to at least 2 relevant pages/products



3–6 headings (H2/H3)



2–4 image suggestions (you can use stock image links or we provide images)



Deliverable B: On-Page Optimisation (2 existing pages per week)



Optimise 2 existing pages per week including:



SEO title + meta description



headings structure



internal links



image alt text updates



readability improvements



Deliverable C: Google Indexing + Search Console Check (weekly)



Confirm newly published content is indexed



Submit URLs for indexing if needed



Identify any errors in Search Console



Provide a short update:



impressions and clicks trend



top queries improving



pages gaining impressions



Deliverable D: Keyword List (weekly)



Provide a list of 10 keyword ideas each week



Include:



keyword



intent (informational / commercial)



recommended page type (blog / product page / landing page)



Deliverable E: Weekly Report (simple, clear)



A short report (1 page max) including:



links to work completed



the keyword targets used



results observed



plan for next week



Monthly Deliverables (After 4 Weeks)



At the end of each month you will deliver:



A list of all pages created/updated



Search Console comparison (month vs previous month):



clicks



impressions



average position



top pages



top queries



A 30-day content plan (titles + keywords)



Required Experience



You must have:



WordPress content publishing experience



On-page SEO experience (RankMath or Yoast)



Ability to use Google Search Console



Previous SEO content writing experience



Strong English writing skills



Ability to follow a structured deliverable schedule



Bonus if you have:



experience with eCommerce SEO



experience in food/beverage/hospitality content



basic Canva or Pinterest experience (optional)



Hours & Pay



Start: 6 hours per week

If performance is strong after 4 weeks: increase to 10 hours per week.



Pay guidance (Philippines):



AUD $5 to $9 per hour depending on experience



If someone claims they are an expert but charges very low, be cautious



We prefer paying a bit more for someone who actually performs



Trial Period



First 2 weeks is a trial.



You will be judged on:



quality of writing



correct SEO structure



ability to follow instructions



speed and reliability



whether your work shows SEO understanding



Application Instructions (Filter)



To apply, you must complete this test:



Suggest 5 SEO blog post titles for an Australian premium mixer brand



For each title, include:



target keyword



search intent (commercial or informational)



Briefly explain how you would use Google Search Console to improve results



Include the word “Tonic” in the first line of your application



Applicants who do not follow instructions will be ignored.



What We Will Provide



our website access



product info and brand voice guidance



the target regions (Australia)



feedback weekly



You should be able to work independently and bring SEO knowledge.

title: Short-Form Video Editor (Fitness + Entrepreneur Content)
description:
Job Type:



Part-Time

Long-Term Opportunity

Remote



About Me



I’m a 23-year-old assistant project manager, civil engineering student, natural bodybuilder, and founder of a clothing brand (ATLA).



I create content around:

• Gym training

• Day-in-the-life (construction + school + fitness)

• Discipline & mindset

• Entrepreneur lifestyle



I am building my personal brand on Instagram and TikTok and looking for a reliable editor to grow with long term.







Responsibilities

 • Edit 2–3 short-form videos per week (30–60 seconds)

 • Create strong hooks in the first 2–3 seconds

 • Add clean, professional captions

 • Edit with good pacing for retention

 • Light cinematic color grading

 • Clean transitions (not flashy or over-edited)

 • Choose background music that fits a disciplined / powerful tone



Style:

Raw documentary + motivational + cinematic







Requirements

 • Experience editing Reels / TikTok

 • Understand short-form algorithm pacing

 • Reliable internet connection

 • Good communication

 • Can deliver on time consistently

 • Open to long-term work



Bonus:

Experience with fitness or lifestyle content.







 Salary



$200 USD per month

3 videos per week

30-day trial period



Opportunity for pay increase based on performance.







To Apply



Please send:

 1. Portfolio (short-form only)

 2. Editing software you use

 3. Your internet speed

 4. Your availability per week

 5. Why you are a good fit for this position



IMPORTANT:

Start your application with the word “DISCIPLINE” so I know you read everything.

title: Customer Experience Professional
description:
Join one of Australia’s Fastest Growing Modern Furniture Brands!



At Life Interiors, we’re passionate about making great design accessible to all. As one Australia’s most rapidly expanding homeware and furniture brand, we’re committed to delivering exceptional experiences for both our customers and our team.



The role



We are seeking a proactive and customer-focused individual to join our team as a Customer Service Representative. This role will emphasize building and maintaining strong, long-term relationships with our customers. The ideal candidate will possess excellent communication skills, a positive attitude, and a passion for ensuring customer satisfaction while fostering loyalty and trust.



What you’ll be doing:



*Customer Relationship Management: Develop and maintain strong, lasting relationships with customers to enhance their

experience with the company. Ensure customers feel valued, heard, and well-supported throughout their journey.

*Issue Resolution: Act as the primary point of contact for customer inquiries, concerns, or issues. Respond promptly and

effectively to ensure customer satisfaction.

*Proactive Outreach: Reach out to existing customers to ensure continued satisfaction, gather feedback, and identify

opportunities for improvements in products or services.

*Customer Retention: Implement strategies and best practices to drive customer retention, resolve complaints effectively,

and maximize overall satisfaction.

*Customer Education: Provide clear and concise information about products or services ensuring customers understand all

offerings and are equipped to make informed decisions.

*Feedback Collection: Collect and analyze customer feedback to identify trends, needs, and areas for improvement,

collaborate with other departments to address concerns.

*Cross-functional Collaboration: Work closely with sales, marketing, and product teams to ensure customers’ needs are met and that customer feedback is communicated effectively to relevant teams.

*Reporting and Documentation: Maintain accurate records of customer interactions, transactions, and feedback using CRM

tools or customer service platforms.



Great to have:



*Independent and able to work minimal training or supervision, quickly learning new tasks and systems.

*Strong attention to detail, skilled at identifying the root cause of customer issues and resolving them accurately in

accordance with established processes.

*Ability to remain focused, effective, and make sound decisions in challenging situations.

*Demonstrate strong time management skills and be able to handle several customer requests or tasks at the same time

without affecting the quality of service.

*Adaptive, can adjust to new priorities, and use new tools or processes easily.

*Assertive and can confidently provide and receive constructive feedback with management in a clear, direct, and respectful manner, without fear of rejection or conflict.

* We also welcome those who were previously employed by Temple and Webster, Lounge Lovers and other Australian Furniture experience (A huge plus!)







What we are looking for:



*Proven experience in customer service, relationship management, or a similar role.

*Excellent communication skills (both verbal and written).

*Strong interpersonal skills with an ability to build rapport with customers and internal teams.

*Ability to handle difficult or sensitive situations with tact and professionalism.

*Problem-solving skills and the ability to think critically and creatively.

*Familiarity with CRM systems and other customer service software is preferred.

*Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.

*Ability to work independently and as part of a team.

*High level of empathy and emotional intelligence to relate to customer needs and concerns.



What We Offer:



*Salary offered: TBD depending on experience

*13th month performance based bonus paid in December

*Annual performance and salary review every June

*HMO with an additional dependent

*10 days Vacation leave

*5 days Sick leave

*3 days additional PTO (Christmas day, New Year’s day, & Good Friday)

*Full-time day shift (9:00 am to 6:00 pm - Australian Eastern Daylight Time (AEDT))

*Permanent work from home set-up



Home office requirements:

iCore 3 or it's equivalent with 4GB Ram

Internet speed should at least be at 50mbps



ONLY SHORT-LISTED CANDIDATES WILL BE CONTACTED



For interested applicants, kindly fill out the application form:




/?k=zLdqBdvRPLt5qwEBbrrYEA&d=

title: Senior Cloud Accountant (Direct Hire / Work from Home)
description:
Join the Team as a Senior  Cloud Accountant at Bean Ninjas USA!

Philippine CPA license highly preferred.



Bean Ninjas USA is on the lookout for enthusiastic and skilled professionals to become part of our expanding team. If you're passionate about cloud accounting and meet the qualifications listed below, we’d love to connect with you!



Filipino candidate must possess the following (Please do not apply if you do not have the following):



~ 3+ years of full-time accounting experience within a BPO or similar environment.

- end-to-end bookkeeping experience including preparation of Balance Sheet and Income Statement

~ Proficient in cloud accounting software and platforms.

~ Strong analytical skills with attention to detail.

~ Excellent English communication skills, both written and verbal.

~ Bachelor's degree in Accounting or related field (preferred)

- Has Xero and/or QuickBooks Online experience



Qualifications for the role:



- Full-time work (Mon-Fri) for our US clients within the Philippines working hours of 8 am to 5 pm.

- We also offer flexibility on the schedule at work once you are familiar with the processes and client work.

- Excellent attention to detail and time management skills.

- Team player who would get along well with the rest of the Bean Ninjas team



Specific Responsibilities



In this role, you will look after management reporting and weekly/monthly transaction processing for a portfolio of Bean Ninjas bookkeeping clients.



Bookkeeping Service Delivery



-Uses Xero and QuickBooks Online

-Uses Xero and QBO add-ons and integrations like Hubdoc, A2X, Receipt Bank, Stripe, and Paypal.

-Responds to client queries via our online
 helpdesk software Helpscout.

-Communicates with other staff via Slack.

-You write well: concise, understandable, helpful, professional, and fast. You can build rapport with others solely through your writing.



Remote work

-You’re self-motivated and ideally, have previous experience working remotely and with little direct supervision.

-You must have an excellent internet connection.

-You know how to keep your computer and work secure and private.

-You know the joys of using two monitors (or splitting a big monitor) to do bookkeeping work.



Who are we?

Bean Ninjas is a bookkeeping and financial reporting business founded on Australia’s Gold Coast in 2015.



We specialize in eCommerce businesses and our mission is to create financial freedom through stress-free business finances for our clients.



We have over 16 Philippine tea
mbers here and we are growing!



What’s it like being part of the Bean Ninjas team?



Career development

We believe in developing our team and promoting internally. You will go through an initial 6-month training program and will then receive ongoing training and mentoring. We prepare individual staff development plans for each tea
mber.



Flexible and remote role

This is a remote role and you will have some level of flexibility in choosing your regular work hours, but must generally be online during Philippine working hours.



Benefits

We offer a variety of benefits from 13th-month pay, birthday vouchers, birthday time off, accrued leaves, and Christmas shutdown leave (where everyone does not need to log in to work for a couple of days during the Christmas shutdown which is usually 5 to 7 working days, yet these days are still paid for by the company!), 6-week maternity leave, remote social activities, and pieces of training and up to date certifications for our accountants on various accounting platforms or integrations.



Bean Ninjas is recognized as an industry leader. You will be working alongside Chartered Accountants and CPAs who have come from firms such as Ernst and Young, BDO, and Grant Thornton.



We won the Xero Australia Bookkeeping Partner of the Year Award in 2019 and the equivalent state award for Queensland in both 2017 and 2019.



Applications can be sent to 
 and include a copy of your resume. Please do not apply if you have no relevant experience or are only looking for a project-based role. Thank you!

title: Looking for 3 Executive Assistants | Earn up to $2000/month
description:
I’m looking for three experienced Executive Assistants to support different parts of my business operations. Each role focuses on keeping things organised behind the scenes — protecting time, managing communication, and making sure tasks and priorities keep moving forward.



These roles suit someone who works with structure, accuracy, and proactive follow-through.



Details:



– Executive Assistant: $500/month | 3 hours per day to start | Fixed UK-time overlap

– Executive Assistant & Operations Partner: $500/month | Part-time | UK hours

– Senior Executive Assistant: $1,500–$2,000/month | Full-time | 6:00 AM – 2:00 PM PH time

– All are Remote work



What You’ll Do



– Manage calendars, scheduling, confirmations, and meeting logistics

– Organise inboxes, prioritise messages, and draft professional
 replies

– Maintain clean task systems with clear owners, due dates, and priorities (ClickUp, Asana, or similar)

– Track follow-ups and ensure nothing stalls due to missing information

– Maintain organised documentation, trackers, templates, and folders

– Convert meeting notes into clear action items and track completion

– Create checklists and systems that reduce repeat mistakes

– Prepare simple reports, trackers, or dashboards when required

– Support research, coordination, or operational tasks when needed

– Help protect priorities by filtering requests and keeping schedules focused



Good Fit If You



– Have Executive Assistant experience supporting founders or senior leaders

– Are highly organised and detail-oriented

– Communicate clearly and professionally in written English

title: Warehouse Sorting Software with Barcode Scanning (CSV Import)
description:
Project Description



We operate a high-volume live auction business via Whatnot. During our live streams we sell thousands of individual items using a generic listing.



Each item sold is assigned a sequential Item Number by the platform.



During the stream we place a sequential barcode sticker on each item (0001, 0002, 0003, etc).



After the show we export a CSV file of orders.



We need a simple software tool that:



Imports the CSV



Assigns each order to a bin



Allows a user to scan item barcodes



Displays which bin the item should go into



This will allow us to quickly sort hundreds of items into order bins for packing.



We then need the packers to be able to scan the items into boxes and have the system reject any items that should not be in that order.   We would also like the ability to track productivity at a user level so it will need to be able to have login/staff accounts.  Web based app preferred that can eventually be sold as a service to other warehouses.

title: Video Editor
description:
FREELANCE VIDEO EDITOR

AI Automation & Agentic AI Content

Remote | Per-Video Contract | Daily Output



About the Role

We're building a content brand in the AI automation, agentic AI, and bespoke AI systems space — and we're looking for a freelance video editor who can keep up with daily publishing across multiple platforms. You'll receive raw footage (a mix of talking-head recordings and software screencasts) and turn it into polished, retention-optimised content ready for YouTube, Instagram Reels, TikTok, LinkedIn, and
.

This is a ground-floor opportunity. We're just launching our content operation, so you'll help shape the look and feel of the brand from day one. We're not after Hollywood production — we want an editor who understands the YouTube education and tech tutorial space, prioritises speed and viewer retention, and can deliver consistent quality at volume. Think clean, punchy, and engaging — not overproduced.

You won't be planning or filming content. We handle that. Your job is to take what we record and make it as watchable, engaging, and professional as possible — fast.



What You'll Be Editing

Long-Form YouTube Content (15–60 min)

These are the flagship videos. A mix of talking-head delivery and screen-recorded walkthroughs of AI tools, automation platforms, and custom system builds. Expect to edit:



Step-by-step tutorials showing how to build AI automations, agents, and workflows inside platforms like Make.com, n8n, or custom code environments

Strategy and explainer videos where the creator talks to camera about AI concepts, business applications, and industry developments

Client case studies and system breakdowns showing real-world AI implementations for businesses

Hybrid videos that switch between talking head and screencast throughout



What good editing looks like here:



Tight jump cuts that remove filler words, pauses, and tangents without making the video feel rushed or choppy

Strategic zoom-ins during screencasts to direct viewer attention to the specific part of the screen that matters — a node in a workflow, a line of code, a settings panel

On-screen text callouts, labels, and annotations that reinforce key points, especially when technical concepts are being explained

Strong intros (first 30 seconds) with faster pacing, pattern interrupts, or a hook that immediately signals value to the viewer

Background music (lo-fi, ambient, or electronic) mixed at appropriate levels under narration, with energy shifts matching content beats

Smooth transitions between talking-head and screencast segments so the video feels like one cohesive piece

Chapter markers and timestamps for longer tutorials



Short-Form Vertical Content (30–90 sec)

Clips repurposed from long-form recordings plus occasional standalone short recordings, distributed across Instagram Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, LinkedIn, and
. This is where most of the daily volume lives.



Identify the most compelling, self-contained moments from longer recordings — strong opinions, surprising insights, punchy tactical advice

Reframe horizontal footage to vertical 9:16 with proper framing of the speaker and any relevant screen content

Add animated/dynamic captions (bold, large, keyword-highlighted) in the style that performs well on Reels and TikTok

Craft hooks in the first 1–2 seconds: text overlays, re-ordered audio, or a punchy opening line pulled from later in the clip

Platform-native pacing — no dead frames, fast cuts, energy from the first second to the last

Adapt aspect ratios and formatting for LinkedIn and
 where vertical or square may perform differently



Thumbnails

One thumbnail per long-form video. These should be bold, high-contrast, and designed for clickthrough: large readable text, expressive creator imagery, and a consistent visual identity we'll develop together as the brand takes shape.



Required Skills



Proficiency in a professional NLE (Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or DaVinci Resolve)

Proven experience editing screencast and tutorial content — you know how to make someone clicking through software actually feel engaging through zooms, callouts, pacing, and visual storytelling

Strong short-form editing skills with a portfolio of Reels/TikTok/Shorts that demonstrate retention-focused techniques (dynamic captions, hooks, fast pacing)

Thumbnail design ability using Canva, Photoshop, or Figma

Audio editing fundamentals: noise reduction, normalisation, music mixing, and handling recordings from varying environments

Ability to independently source royalty-free background music that fits a tech/education brand

Understanding of YouTube retention mechanics — you know why the first 30 seconds matter, how to structure pattern interrupts, and what makes viewers stay

Reliable and communicative — daily output means we need someone who delivers on time, every time, and flags issues early





Nice to Have



Familiarity with AI and automation tools (Make.com, n8n, Zapier, LangChain, custom AI agents) — if you understand what's on screen, you'll make smarter editing decisions about where to cut and what to emphasise

Experience with After Effects or Motion for lightweight motion graphics (animated lower-thirds, intro sequences, transitions)

Experience editing for LinkedIn — it's a different audience and tone compared to TikTok/Reels, and understanding that nuance is valuable

Comfort scaling up — we're starting here, but this role will grow as the brand grows, and we want someone who can grow with us

Familiarity with YouTube Analytics and audience retention graphs — being able to look at performance data and adjust your editing approach is a major plus





Expected Volume (to Start)



Long-form YouTube: 3–5 edited videos per week (first drafts within 24–48 hours of receiving footage)

Short-form clips: 5–7 per week across Reels, TikTok, Shorts, LinkedIn, and

Thumbnails: 1 per long-form video

Revisions: 1–2 rounds on long-form; shorts and thumbnails should be near-final on first delivery





How We'll Work Together

The workflow is straightforward. We record, you edit.



We upload raw footage (talking head, screencast, or both) to a shared drive with a brief note on the video's topic and any specific requests

You edit, add music, callouts, captions, and any necessary graphics

You deliver the edited video plus thumbnail to the shared drive

We review, provide feedback if needed, and you deliver the final version

For short-form: you identify the best clips from long-form footage and deliver batches of edited vertical content on a regular cadence



Communication will be via Slack,
, or WhatsApp — whatever works. We value editors who are proactive, flag potential issues early, and occasionally suggest improvements rather than waiting to be told.



How to Apply



Portfolio or reel showing tech/tutorial editing work — we want to see both long-form and short-form examples. Screencast editing samples are essential.

A sample edit: Find any AI/tech tutorial on YouTube, pick a 3–5 minute segment, and re-edit it the way you'd edit for us. Show us your pacing, your callouts, your zooms, and your style. Then cut a 60-second vertical short from the same footage.

Your rate (per long-form video and per short-form clip) and your realistic weekly capacity.

Your timezone and typical turnaround time for a 30–45 minute tutorial edit.





We're building something from scratch and want an editor who's excited to grow with us. If you can make a 45-minute screencast feel like 10 minutes and turn a single recording into a week's worth of shorts, we want to hear from you.

title: YouTube Faceless Video Editor - History/Mystery/Scary Documentary
description:
Hi! I’m hiring a video editor for a faceless YouTube channel focused on history / mystery / scary documentary-style storytelling (serious, informative, suspenseful — not comedic).

Paid trial (pilot):



We start with 1 paid pilot video

Ongoing (after pilot):



If the pilot is a fit, we continue on a monthly part-time retainer: $1,000/month.



Expected output (target): up to 6 videos/month (effectively $200/video).



If fewer videos are produced in a month, pay is pro-rated at $200 per completed video.



Video style (what I’m looking for)



Mostly images + archival visuals with smooth zooms/pans, moving canvases (documentary feel)



Professional motion graphics where needed (clean, modern, not template/beginner-looking)

I will provide



Final script

25 fps preferred (or keep a consistent project frame rate)

Please clarify whether your edit includes:

music/SFX sourcing (licensed or royalty-free)



map + timeline visuals and the level of motion graphics included

what’s included in your work(maps, sourcing, sound design, revisions)



Your turnaround time



Software you use (Premiere/After Effects / DaVinci Resolve, etc.)



Any questions you have about scope



Thank you!

This role ensures accurate financial records, timely statutory filings, and full compliance with Philippine government regulations across all business units.

________________________________________

Scope of Work

This scope of work covers ALL branches of Saiko Restaurant and Laurivia Jewelry and Accessories Shop.

________________________________________

Key Responsibilities:

1. Bookkeeping & Financial Records

• Prepare and maintain customized Excel Books of Accounts for all branches, including:

o Cash Receipts Book

o Cash Disbursement Book

o Purchase Book

o Sales Book

o Journal Book

o Payroll Summary with payslips

• Generate consolidated and per-branch monthly and quarterly financial reports:

o Profit & Loss Statement

o Balance Sheet

o Statement of Cash Flows

• Prepare templates for vouchers, liquidation reports, and ageing of receivables/payables, when necessary.

• Implement and monitor voucher system for all cash disbursements to ensure proper control and documentation.

________________________________________

2. Payroll Processing & Government Remittances

• Process payroll on a biweekly basis for all branches.

• Collect and review Daily Time Records (DTR) three (3) days before payroll release.

• Prepare a draft payroll summary one (1) to two (2) days before the scheduled payroll date.

• Ensure that the drafted payroll summary is reviewed and approved by the HR Manager and Branch Owners prior to payroll release.

• Prepare individual payslips and payroll summary reports.

• Compute, withhold, and remit mandatory government contributions, including:

o Social Security System (SSS)

o Philippine Health Insurance Corporation (PhilHealth / PHIC)

o Home Development Mutual Fund (HDMF / Pag-IBIG)

• Address employee concerns related to government benefits, including SSS salary loan applications and other statutory inquiries.

________________________________________

3. BIR Compliance & Tax Filing

• Prepare, file, pay, and properly store all monthly, quarterly, and annual BIR returns for all branches, including but not limited to:

o Quarterly Income Tax Return (1701Q)

o VAT Return (2550Q)

o Annual Income Tax Return (1701)

o BIR Form 2316

• Prepare and submit required attachments such as:

o VAT RELIEF

o Alphalist of Suppliers

o Annual Alphalist of Compensation

o Annual Inventory List

• Issue BIR Form 2307 to suppliers.

• Compile and monitor creditable withholding tax certificates received from customers.

• Coordinate with external auditors if required (excluding annual audit fees).

________________________________________

4. Management Reporting & Financial Advisory

• Provide branch-level and consolidated financial analysis.

• Participate in financial planning and budgeting for both business entities.

• Provide consultation on cost monitoring, pricing strategy, and financial performance improvement.

________________________________________

5. Business Permit & Regulatory Assistance

• Assist in the annual renewal of business permits for all branches.

• Prepare and provide required supporting documents for permit processing.

________________________________________

Qualifications:

• Bachelor’s Degree in Accountancy, Accounting Technology, or related field.

• Proven experience in multi-branch bookkeeping and payroll processing.

• Knowledgeable in Philippine taxation and statutory compliance.

• Proficient in Microsoft Excel and financial report preparation.

• Highly organized, detail-oriented, and capable of handling multiple entities simultaneously.

title: Experienced Graphic Designer / Photo Retoucher
description:
We’re hiring an experienced Graphic Designer and Photo Retoucher with real estate experience who understands how to create clean, professional, high-converting visuals across digital and print.



This role is for someone who knows how to design with clarity and purpose. You understand branding, layout, hierarchy, and how visuals influence attention and trust, especially in real estate marketing.



—-----------------------------------------------------------------------------



READ THE ENTIRE JOB LISTING CAREFULLY. The final step of the hiring process will require the candidate to provide at least 1 former employer/team lead reference and at least 1 character reference. These will be verified prior to extending an offer.



--- ABOUT THE TEAM ---

Want to work in a rewarding, fast-paced, start-up atmosphere? Looking for significant growth and performance bonus opportunities? Have a growth mindset, value relationships, and dedicated to getting results? Then we might be a fit!



What is it like to work with us? HEAR FROM OUR TEAM!   





Our Team Core Values:

1) DELIGHT THE CLIENT:  We consistently exceed expectations by anticipating needs, delivering outstanding service, and creating those “magic 
nts” where every client feels seen and heard.

2) MASTER YOUR CRAFT:  We commit to continuous learning, share our expertise openly, and tackle every challenge with precision and a deep sense of duty.

3) FOLLOW THROUGH, ALWAYS:  When we say something will happen, it does—every time. We own every task, ensuring that nothing falls through the cracks, and when mistakes occur, we learn and improve immediately.



Our core values are important, and the right candidate will share our values. We are building an awesome place to work, not just managing marketing campaigns for our real estate business.



--- ABOUT YOU ---

Do you have real estate design experience and understand what makes listings visually credible and market-ready? Do you know how to design social posts and brochures that look clean, professional, and on-brand without overcomplicating them? Do you move fast when timelines are tight and still pay attention to spacing, alignment, and small details?



Then keep reading to see if we're a fit!



Responsibilities:

Social & Digital Design

- Design YouTube branding assets including thumbnails, banners, and episode graphics.

- Create Instagram listing posts, announcements, ads, and story graphics.

- Develop and maintain branded social templates for consistent output.



Print Design

- Design real estate yard signs and signage.

- Create listing brochures and marketing flyers.

- Prepare clean, print-ready files with correct sizing and bleed settings.



Real Estate Photo Editing

- Edit listing photos for lighting, color correction, and clarity.

- Clean up images by removing distractions and enhancing presentation.

- Ensure final images look professional while remaining realistic.



Brand Consistency & Organization

- Maintain consistent fonts, colors, and layout standards

- Ensure all visuals align with company branding

- Organize design files clearly for future use



Requirements:

- Proven experience in real estate graphic design, required

- Strong portfolio showing real estate social and print work, required

- Experience with Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, Lightroom), or similar tools

- Strong understanding of layout, spacing, and visual hierarchy

- Experience preparing print-ready files

- Experience editing real estate photos

- Ability to work quickly without sacrificing quality

- Strong communication skills

- Ability to follow brand guidelines

- A stable, high-speed internet connection

- Growth mindset



BONUS SKILLS (NOT REQUIRED)

- Light motion graphics

- Understanding of social media performance metrics

- Basic copy awareness (headline spacing, readability, CTA placement)



NOTE: This is a project-based role with the opportunity to grow into a full-time position for the right person.



--- APPLICATION & RECRUITMENT PROCESS ---

STEP 1:

Fill out the application form through the link below and write "green kangaroo" in the Additional Comments field.







STEP 2:

Qualified candidates may be invited to participate in two PAID test projects, $10-20 USD for each test project estimated to be 2-3hrs of work for each (a total of $20-40 USD!).



STEP 3:

Candidates who advance through the test project rounds will be invited to an interview.



STEP 4:

Before extending a trial offer, we will conduct a CHARACTER REFERENCE CHECK for the final candidate(s). CONDUCTING A REFERENCE CHECK will be the last step in the hiring process.



STEP 5:

Upon receiving a trial offer, you will be onboarded as a Graphic Designer with a 90-day PAID trial period. During this period, we expect you to demonstrate your editing expertise, meet project deadlines, be a team player, and excel at what you do. A successful trial will lead to a permanent offer to join the team.

title: VO Artist  — History/Mystery/Scary Documentary Faceless YouTube
description:
Hi! I’m hiring a voice-over artist for a faceless YouTube channel focused on history / mystery / scary documentary-style stories.



Style needed: calm, serious documentary tone with subtle suspense (not overly dramatic), clear pronunciation, and good pacing.



Paid trial (pilot):



We start with 1 paid pilot video



Pilot pay: $200 for the completed pilot (includes 1–2 revision rounds).



Ongoing (after pilot):



If the pilot is a fit, we continue on a monthly part-time retainer: $900/month.



Expected output (target): up to 6 videos/month (effectively $150/video).



If fewer videos are produced in a month, pay is pro-rated at $150 per completed video.



Audio requirements (important)



Clean recording (no echo / noise / hiss)



Consistent volume, no clipping



Light cleanup is fine — keep it natural



Deliverables



WAV preferred (48kHz / 24-bit)



Optional: MP3 320kbps as an extra



Revisions



Please include 1 pickup round for small fixes (misreads/pronunciation), up to ~2–3 minutes of pickups.



Turnaround:



Turnaround: ideally 48–72 hours after receiving the script



To apply (required)



Start your proposal with “Midnight” and include:



A demo that matches documentary narration (or offer a short custom sample)



what’s included (cleanup, WAV delivery, pickups)



Your typical turnaround time



Shortlisted candidates will receive voice/tone references (including a channel-style benchmark) and may be asked for a 30–60 second custom sample from the script to confirm fit.



Thank you

title: Product Assistant Role
description:
Join one of Australia's fastest-growing travel businesses!



We're a dynamic and successful team that's been making waves in the travel industry for the past four years. We have a fantastic, high-energy work culture and a team that is focused on success—we're breaking new records every week and month!



This is a ground-floor opportunity to build an incredible career in travel. As we continue to grow, you'll be in a great position to grow with us.



???? About the Role



We are seeking a highly meticulous and organised Product Assistant to join our busy team.

In this critical role, you will be the backbone of our product offerings. You will be responsible for ensuring all our amazing travel packages are accurately loaded, maintained, and priced in our systems. Your work will directly impact what our customers see and book, making your attention to detail paramount to our success.

You will be working with our internal booking system and Google Sheets daily.



Key Responsibilities

- Accurately upload new travel packages, including descriptions, inclusions, and images, into our system.

- Maintain and update existing travel packages with new travel dates, itinerary changes, and stop-sells.

- Carefully review and interpret supplier contracts to extract key information (e.g., rates, blackout dates, terms).

- Upload and manage complex pricing structures, ensuring 100?curacy.

- Conduct regular checks and audits to ensure all live pricing and availability are correct and up-to-date.

- Liaise with travel service providers to assist in uploading new travel packages and maintaining existing ones.

- Manage and organise product data using Google Sheets.

- Assist the wider product and operations team with various administrative tasks as required.



About You



To be successful in this role, you must be a systems-oriented person who thrives on accuracy.



? Non-Negotiables (Must-Haves):

- Exceptional Attention to Detail: You must be meticulous, spotting errors that others might miss. This is the most important quality for this role.

- Google Sheets Proficiency: You are highly confident working with spreadsheets, including data entry, basic formulas, and data filtering.

- Excellent English Skills: You have strong written and verbal communication skills.

- An Organised Mindset: You can manage multiple tasks and prioritise effectively in a fast-paced environment.



? Bonus Points (Nice-to-Haves):



Travel Industry Experience: A background in the travel industry (especially Australian travel) is a huge plus!

Australian Business Experience: Familiarity with working for an Australian-based company.

Systems Experience: Any previous experience with booking systems or databases is highly regarded.

title: General Virtual Assistant (Clothing Brand)
description:
How to Apply – 3-Minute Creative Video Application

Record a 3-minute video covering these points:



1) Your Edge (Best Skills)

What are your top 2–3 skills that make you dangerous in a creative role?

What do people usually rely on you for? (e.g., visuals, editing, ideas, ops, speed, problem-solving)



2) Show Your Work (Creative Proof)

Walk us through 1–2 pieces from your portfolio (designs, videos, content, pages, systems, etc.)

Explain why you made them that way and what problem they solved

Drop links or screen-record your work if needed



3) How You Think (Creative Process)

Pick one project and explain how you go from idea ? execution

How do you come up with concepts? How do you improve weak creative?



4) Your Work Style (Execution Energy)

How do you handle feedback, tight deadlines, and messy tasks?

What does “doing the work” look like to you when motivation is low?



5) Optional Flex (If You Have It)

Any results you can share (growth, views, CTR, conversions, systems improved)

Any tools you’re especially strong with (e.g., Shopify, CapCut, Canva, Notion, Figma, etc.)



Quick Notes (Optional to include):

We’re looking for people who can think and execute

Show us how you solve problems, not just what you’ve done

Raw > perfect. Clarity > hype.



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------



We’re Hiring: General Virtual Assistant (Clothing Brand)

We’re building a fast-growing e-commerce brand and looking for a General Virtual Assistant to support the General Manager with daily operations.

This is not a one-task VA role.

You’ll touch multiple areas: ops, basic design, social coordination, HR support, customer support backup, and Shopify store tasks — helping keep the business running smoothly day-to-day.

If you’re organized, proactive, and comfortable wearing multiple hats, this role can grow into a long-term core team position.



What You’ll Drive

Smooth day-to-day operations

Tasks delivered on time without reminders

Faster execution across ops, HR, CS/FF support, and store management

Clear task tracking and follow-through

Reduced bottlenecks across teams



What You’ll Do

Support the General Manager with daily tasks

Coordinate basic operations and follow-ups

Assist with simple graphic design (Canva/Figma)

Help with social media posting & coordination

Support basic HR tasks (candidate tracking, scheduling interviews, onboarding docs)

Act as backup for Customer Support / Fulfillment (ticket triage, order issues, simple follow-ups)

Handle basic Shopify store tasks (product uploads, descriptions, tagging, collections, basic edits)

Organize assets, files, and trackers

Update reports and task boards

Communicate with internal tea
mbers to unblock tasks



You’re a Fit If You:

Have worked with e-commerce brands before

Are organized, reliable, and detail-oriented

Can manage multiple tasks without dropping details

Have basic graphic design skills (Canva / Figma)

Understand basic social media workflows

Can support HR coordination tasks

Are comfortable backing up CS / fulfillment when needed

Have basic Shopify experience

Follow SOPs and checklists without being reminded

Communicate clearly and respond on time



Bonus:

Experience with Shopify admin

Experience with CS tools (Gorgias, Zendesk, HelpScout, etc.)

Experience with fulfillment workflows

Worked with founders or ops managers

Task management tools (Notion, ClickUp, Asana, Sheets)

title: Front-End Developer (React / Next.js / CSS)
description:
Front-End Developer (React / Next.js / CSS) 



Role: Front-End Developer

Stack: React.js, Next.js, CSS 

Experience: Minimum 2 years professional experience 

Engagement: Hourly contract

Pay: Quote your hourly rate in AUD

Payment: Must have a WISE account (or be willing to set one up)

Access: GitHub access only (repo-based permissions, no server/production access)



What you’ll build (current scope)



 • we have design and layout --> its mostly clean up and get standardise pixel / colour accurate (size, weight, font, padding, Mobile friendly) 



Requirements

 • Build responsive UI components from provided instructions (Monique will provide the step-by-step)

 • Clean, maintainable component structure

 • Form validation + good UX (loading states, success/error messages)



Deliverables



 • Components/pages implemented in react/ Next.js

 • CSS styling aligned with existing design patterns

 • Short handover notes in the repo (README section or /docs)



Working style

 • Daily/regular updates in GitHub (comments + progress notes)

 • Must be comfortable with written instructions from Monique (via teams and google sheets)



To apply 

 1. Link 1–3 relevant projects (React/Next/CSS)

 2. Confirm years of experience (must be 2+)

 3. Confirm hourly rate in AUD

 4. Confirm you have WISE

 5. Confirm you’re OK with GitHub-only access

 6. Your availability (hours/week + time zone (AEDT - Sydney)



?



GitHub “Access Only” Setup (simple + safe)

 • Add contractor as Outside Collaborator (or org member with limited role)

 • Permissions: Read + Write (only if they need to push branches)

 • Protect main branch:

 • PR required

 • At least 1 approval required

 • No direct pushes to main

 • Share secrets/env via your internal team only (contractor never gets prod keys)



?



Payment Rule (WISE)

 • Candidate must invoice weekly/fortnightly with:

 • Hours worked

 • Links to PRs/issues completed

 • Hourly rate (AUD)

 • You pay via WISE in AUD (or convert if needed)

title: Affiliate Manager
description:
Job Description:



Full-Time | Work From Home | Ecommerce & Influencer Partnerships



SRI Labs is a fast-growing, premium beauty & wellness ecommerce brand known for high-end skincare, hair tools, and breakthrough consumer products. We’re looking for a driven, organized, and relationship-focused Affiliate Manager to lead and scale our affiliate program across platforms like ShopMy, Levanta, and key influencer networks.



If you love partnering with creators, optimizing affiliate performance, and helping a brand grow through relationships — this is the role for you.



What You’ll Do



As our Affiliate Manager, you will own the entire affiliate ecosystem and work directly with the CEO and marketing leadership team to scale creator-led revenue.



Key Responsibilities



Manage and optimize SRI Labs’ affiliate program across ShopMy, Levanta, and other affiliate platforms.

Recruit, vet, and onboard new affiliates, creators, and influencers that align with our brand.

Build strong long-term relationships with partners to drive consistent revenue and brand growth.

Track performance, payouts, ROI, and ensure all affiliates are supported with assets, codes, and campaigns.

Coordinate affiliate-driven launches, seasonal promotions, and strategic campaigns.

Identify top performers and build incentive strategies to maximize their output.

Maintain reporting dashboards and communicate results to leadership weekly.

Collaborate with internal marketing, product, design, and operations teams to support affiliate needs.

Continuously test new platforms, creators, strategies, and incentives to grow revenue.

Who You Are



We’re looking for someone who is:



Based in the Philippines

Experienced in affiliate, influencer marketing, or digital partnerships (1–3+ years preferred).

Organized, reliable, and able to manage many creators and campaigns at once.

Strong at relationship-building — someone affiliates love to work with.

Comfortable working within platforms like ShopMy, Levanta, Impact, or similar tools.

Data-driven with excellent follow-through and communication skills.

Hungry, proactive, and passionate about helping a brand grow.

Why Join SRI Labs?



Remote work

Work directly with a high-performing founding team

Competitive salary based on experience

Long-term growth potential within a fast-scaling ecommerce company

Supportive, positive culture built around speed, innovation, and passion for great products

Opportunity to make a big impact — your work will directly drive revenue and brand expansion

How to Apply



 your resume and a quick 2–3 sentence introduction 



Subject Line: Affiliate Manager – [Your Name]



Include:



Links to affiliate programs or creators you’ve worked with

Any relevant platforms/tools you’ve used

Your expected monthly salary (in PHP)

Job Type: Full-time
